zh.lifeSummary: 當William Sinclair 1st Lord Rosslyn於大約1230年出生在法國諾門迪時，他的父親Robert Sinclair41歲，母親Eleanor de Dreux44歲。他和Amicia de Roskelyn至少有5個兒子和3個女兒。他於1296年登記服兵役。他於1271年被列為sheriff of edinburgh。他去世於1297年在英國英格蘭米德萊可堂爾 本 倫敦享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: Sinclair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Clair-sur-Elle (Manche), so called from the dedication of its church to Saint Clarus (see   Clare   3).
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

History: The Scottish family Sinclair (see 1 above) came to southern Scotland in the twelfth century from Huntingdonshire with the de Morvilles. Descendants of these Sinclairs became earls of Caithness in the 15th century. The frequency of the surname in Caithness and Orkney is largely due to the adoption by tenants of the name of their overlord.

variants.name: Clare
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: St. Clair
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Sinclair  . This is also a French Canadian and American French altered form of the same surname.
French (Saint-Clair): habitational name from any of several places, e.g. in Normandy, named Saint-Clair (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Clarus). Compare Saintclair .

Haitian (also Saint-Clair): ornamental name or nickname, perhaps ironic, composed of French saint ‘saint’ or ‘holy’ and clair ‘clear’ or ‘lightness’. Alternatively, perhaps of the same French origin as 2 above. Compare Saintclair .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When William Sinclair 1st Lord Rosslyn was born about 1230, in Normandy, France, his father, Robert Sinclair, was 41 and his mother, Eleanor de Dreux, was 44. He had at least 5 sons and 3 daughters with Amicia de Roskelyn. He registered for military service in 1296. In 1271, his occupation is listed as sheriff of edinburgh. He died in 1297, in Tower of London, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Sinclair 1st Lord Rosslyn est né environ 1230, à Normandie, France, son père, Robert Sinclair, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Eleanor de Dreux, avait 44 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles avec Amicia de Roskelyn. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1296. En 1271, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of edinburgh. Il est décédé en 1297, à Tower of London, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 68 ans.

Er hatte mit Amicia de Roskelyn mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er hat sich 1296 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1271 ist sein Beruf als sheriff of edinburgh aufgeführt. Er starb 1297 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Tower of London, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie con Amicia de Roskelyn. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1296. Nel 1271, la sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of edinburgh. Morì nel 1297, nel Tower of London, Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 68 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: William Sinclair 1st Lord Rosslyn(이)가 대략 1230년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Sinclair(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor de Dreux(은)는 44세였다. 그는 Amicia de Roskelyn(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1296년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1271년, 그의 직업은 sheriff of edinburgh이었다. 그는 1297년 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 타워 프 런던에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas com Amicia de Roskelyn. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1296. Em 1271, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of edinburgh. Ele faleceu em 1297, em Tower of London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Sinclair 1st Lord Rosslyn родился около 1230 г. в местности норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Robert Sinclair было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Eleanor de Dreux, 44. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери от Amicia de Roskelyn. В 1296 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1271 годуработал по профессии sheriff of edinburgh. Он умер в 1297 г. в местности тоуир оф лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 68 лет.
